pem.c(3) Library Functions Manual pem.c(3)

NAME

pem.c - 📄 PEM format encoding/decoding utilities for certificates and keys (adapted from BearSSL)

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct bvector
Dynamic byte vector for PEM decoding. struct pem_object_t
Parsed PEM object with name and data.

Macros


#define VECTOR(type)
#define VEC_INIT {0, 0, 0}
#define VEC_CLEAR(vec)
#define VEC_CLEAREXT(vec, fun)
#define VEC_ADD(vec, x)
#define VEC_ADDMANY(vec, xp, num)
#define VEC_ELT(vec, idx)
#define VEC_LEN(vec)
#define VEC_TOARRAY(vec)

Functions


void free_ta_contents (br_x509_trust_anchor *ta)
size_t read_trust_anchors_from_memory (anchor_list *dst, const unsigned char *pem_data, size_t pem_len)

Detailed Description

📄 PEM format encoding/decoding utilities for certificates and keys (adapted from BearSSL)

Definition in file pem.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define VEC_ADD( vec, x)

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  (vec).buf = vector_expand((vec).buf, sizeof *((vec).buf), &(vec).ptr, &(vec).len, 1);                              \


  (vec).buf[(vec).ptr++] = (x);                                                                                      \
} while (0)




Definition at line 68 of file pem.c.


68 #define VEC_ADD(vec, x)                                                                                                \
69   do {                                                                                                                 \
70     (vec).buf = vector_expand((vec).buf, sizeof *((vec).buf), &(vec).ptr, &(vec).len, 1);                              \
71     (vec).buf[(vec).ptr++] = (x);                                                                                      \
72   } while (0)






Referenced by read_trust_anchors_from_memory().






#define
  VEC_ADDMANY( vec, xp, num)


Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  size_t vec_num = (num);                                                                                            \


  (vec).buf = vector_expand((vec).buf, sizeof *((vec).buf), &(vec).ptr, &(vec).len, vec_num);                        \


  memcpy((vec).buf + (vec).ptr, (xp), vec_num * sizeof *((vec).buf));                                                \


  (vec).ptr += vec_num;                                                                                              \
} while (0)






Definition at line 74 of file pem.c.


74 #define VEC_ADDMANY(vec, xp, num)                                                                                      \
75   do {                                                                                                                 \
76     size_t vec_num = (num);                                                                                            \
77     (vec).buf = vector_expand((vec).buf, sizeof *((vec).buf), &(vec).ptr, &(vec).len, vec_num);                        \
78     memcpy((vec).buf + (vec).ptr, (xp), vec_num * sizeof *((vec).buf));                                                \
79     (vec).ptr += vec_num;                                                                                              \
80   } while (0)

#define
  VEC_CLEAR( vec)


Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  SAFE_FREE((vec).buf);                                                                                              \


  (vec).buf = NULL;                                                                                                  \


  (vec).ptr = 0;                                                                                                     \


  (vec).len = 0;                                                                                                     \
} while (0)

#define
  VEC_CLEAREXT( vec, fun)


Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  size_t vec_tmp;                                                                                                    \


  for (vec_tmp = 0; vec_tmp < (vec).ptr; vec_tmp++) {                                                                \


    (fun)(&(vec).buf[vec_tmp]);                                                                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \


  VEC_CLEAR(vec);                                                                                                    \
} while (0)






Definition at line 59 of file pem.c.


59 #define VEC_CLEAREXT(vec, fun)                                                                                         \
60   do {                                                                                                                 \
61     size_t vec_tmp;                                                                                                    \
62     for (vec_tmp = 0; vec_tmp < (vec).ptr; vec_tmp++) {                                                                \
63       (fun)(&(vec).buf[vec_tmp]);                                                                                      \
64     }                                                                                                                  \
65     VEC_CLEAR(vec);                                                                                                    \
66   } while (0)

Value:

  size_t ptr, len;                                                                                                   \

Function
  Documentation




void
  free_ta_contents (br_x509_trust_anchor * ta)


Definition at line 428 of file pem.c.


428                                                 {
429   if (!ta) {
430     return;
431   }
432   xfree(ta->dn.data);
433   switch (ta->pkey.key_type) {
434   case BR_KEYTYPE_RSA:
435     xfree((void *)ta->pkey.key.rsa.n);
436     xfree((void *)ta->pkey.key.rsa.e);
437     break;
438   case BR_KEYTYPE_EC:
439     xfree((void *)ta->pkey.key.ec.q);
440     break;
441   default:
442     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Unknown public key type in CA");
443     break;
444   }
445 }






References SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by https_get(), and
    read_trust_anchors_from_memory().






size_t
  read_trust_anchors_from_memory (anchor_list * dst, const unsigned char *
  pem_data, size_t pem_len)


Definition at line 449 of file pem.c.


449                                                                                                        {
450   br_x509_certificate *xcs;
451   anchor_list tas = VEC_INIT;
452   size_t u, num;
453 
454   xcs = read_certificates_from_memory(pem_data, pem_len, &num);
455   if (xcs == NULL) {
456     return 0;
457   }
458 
459   for (u = 0; u < num; u++) {
460     br_x509_trust_anchor ta;
461 
462     if (certificate_to_trust_anchor_inner(&ta, &xcs[u]) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
463       VEC_CLEAREXT(tas, free_ta_contents);
464       free_certificates(xcs, num);
465       return 0;
466     }
467     VEC_ADD(tas, ta);
468   }
469 
470   VEC_ADDMANY(*dst, &VEC_ELT(tas, 0), num);
471   VEC_CLEAR(tas);
472   free_certificates(xcs, num);
473   return num;
474 }






References free_ta_contents(), VEC_ADD,
    VEC_ADDMANY, VEC_CLEAR, VEC_CLEAREXT, VEC_ELT,
    and VEC_INIT.


Referenced by https_get().
